description: An entry for a permission value in a [`Capability`] can be either a raw permission [`Identifier`] or an object that references a permission and extends its scope.

anyOf.description: Reference a permission or permission set by identifier.
Reference a permission or permission set by identifier and extends its scope.

This permission set configures which
shell functionality is exposed by default.

#### Granted Permissions

It allows to use the `open` functionality with a reasonable
scope pre-configured. It will allow opening `http(s)://`,
`tel:` and `mailto:` links.

#### This default permission set includes:

- `allow-open`
anyOf.markdownDescription:
This permission set configures which
shell functionality is exposed by default.

#### Granted Permissions

It allows to use the `open` functionality with a reasonable
scope pre-configured. It will allow opening `http(s)://`,
`tel:` and `mailto:` links.

#### This default permission set includes:

- `allow-open`

anyOf.description: Enables the execute command without any pre-configured scope.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enables the execute command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Enables the kill command without any pre-configured scope.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enables the kill command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Enables the open command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Enables the spawn command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Enables the stdin_write command without any pre-configured scope.
anyOf.markdownDescription: Enables the stdin_write command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Denies the execute command without any pre-configured scope.

anyOf.description: Denies the stdin_write command without any pre-configured scope.

args.description: The allowed arguments for the command execution.

cmd.description: The command name. It can start with a variable that resolves to a system base directory. The variables are: `$AUDIO`, `$CACHE`, `$CONFIG`, `$DATA`, `$LOCALDATA`, `$DESKTOP`, `$DOCUMENT`, `$DOWNLOAD`, `$EXE`, `$FONT`, `$HOME`, `$PICTURE`, `$PUBLIC`, `$RUNTIME`, `$TEMPLATE`, `$VIDEO`, `$RESOURCE`, `$LOG`, `$TEMP`, `$APPCONFIG`, `$APPDATA`, `$APPLOCALDATA`, `$APPCACHE`, `$APPLOG`.

name.description:
The name for this allowed shell command configuration.

This name will be used inside of the webview API to call this command along with any specified arguments.

identifier.description: Identifier of the permission or permission set.
Identifier of the permission or permission set.

allow.description: Data that defines what is allowed by the scope.

deny.description: Data that defines what is denied by the scope. This should be prioritized by validation logic.
